summary: The Docker build (bake) failed because a `wget` command inside the Dockerfile received an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway response from GitHub when trying to download the nydus release tarball. This is a transient network error that caused the build step to exit with code 1, aborting the entire bake.
root_cause: During the Docker bake build, the `nydus` stage ran `wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/v2.4.0/nydus-static-v2.4.0-linux-amd64.tgz` and received `HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error` from GitHub, causing the process to exit with code 1 and failing the build.

evidence.quote: #72 0.149 wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: The wget command received a 502 Bad Gateway from GitHub, which is a transient network/server error.

evidence.quote: #72 ERROR: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: The wget failure caused the Docker build step to exit with code 1, propagating the error.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: GitHub Actions reported the final bake failure, confirming the root cause was the failed wget in the nydus build stage.
Identifies the exact Dockerfile line (line 336) that triggered the failure.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient 502 Bad Gateway error from GitHub's release download endpoint. If the issue persists, consider adding retry logic to the wget command in Dockerfile line 336 (e.g., `wget --tries=3`) or mirroring the nydus release artifact.

summary: The test `test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with the same network error, indicating a transient GitHub availability issue.
root_cause: GitHub returned repeated 502 errors when the test attempted to download https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip, causing pip to exhaust its retries and exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from pip showing that GitHub returned too many 502 errors, exhausting all retries — a transient network/server-side failure.
The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with the same 502 error, confirming this is a persistent transient network issue rather than a code defect.
The final test result shows the test failed after all retries were exhausted, with the root cause being the network failure to reach GitHub.

evidence.quote: 1 failed, 1080 passed, 44 skipped, 21 xfailed, 2 xpassed, 5 warnings, 3 rerun in 451.05s (0:07:31)
evidence.reason: Only 1 test failed out of 1080 passed, and it was the one making a live network request to GitHub, strongly indicating a transient external network issue rather than a code problem.

suggested_fix: Re-run the CI job, as this is a transient GitHub 502 network failure. Consider mocking the network call or using a local fixture for the test to avoid flakiness from external service availability.

summary: A single Jest test assertion failed in `test/config/get.ts`. The test `pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc` expected the registry URL `https://my-org.registry.example.com` (without a trailing slash) but received `https://my-org.registry.example.com/` (with a trailing slash), indicating that `pnpm config get` is now appending a trailing slash to scoped registry URLs.
root_cause: The `pnpm config get` command returns a registry URL with a trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`) when the test expects it without one (`https://my-org.registry.example.com`). The behavior of the `@my-org:registry` config value output changed to include a trailing slash, causing the `toBe` equality check to fail.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.reason: This is the direct assertion mismatch — the received value has a trailing slash that the test does not expect.
This is the exact line (test/config/get.ts:32) where the assertion fails, confirming the test expects no trailing slash.
Confirms the test suite that failed.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Confirms this is the only failing test, isolating the root cause to this single assertion.
Confirms the Jest run exited with code 1 due to the test failure, propagating the CI failure.

suggested_fix: Update the test assertion in `test/config/get.ts` at line 32 to expect the trailing slash (`https://my-org.registry.example.com/`), or fix the `pnpm config get` implementation to strip the trailing slash from scoped registry URLs before outputting them.

args.pattern: pnpm config get reads npm options
